sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value1 FLOAT, value2 FLOAT, category VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value1, value2, category) VALUES (1, NULL, 2.5, 'A'), (2, 3.5, 4.5, 'B'), (3, 1.5, NULL, 'C');
SELECT REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) FROM test_data GROUP BY category HAVING REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) <> 2.0;
SELECT id, REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) AS intercept FROM test_data WHERE intercept <> 2.0 ORDER BY intercept DESC;
CREATE TABLE temp_data AS SELECT id, REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) as intercept FROM test_data WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL AND value2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT * FROM temp_data WHERE intercept IS NOT NULL AND intercept <> (SELECT AVG(intercept) FROM temp_data);
SELECT DISTINCT a.id FROM test_data a JOIN test_data b ON a.id <> b.id WHERE REGR_INTERCEPT(a.value1, b.value2) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) <> (SELECT MAX(REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2)) FROM test_data);
SELECT category, REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) FROM test_data GROUP BY category HAVING REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) IS NOT NULL AND REGR_INTERCEPT(value1, value2) <> 1.0;
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value1, value2, category) VALUES (4, 5.6, NULL, 'D');
